mnemonics.title: Five Framework Activities
mnemonics.content: Remember: Communication → Planning → Modeling → Construction → Deployment. Mnemonic: 'Cute Pandas Can't Jump' (Communication, Planning, Construction, Modeling, Deployment - rearranged alphabetically)

mnemonics.title: Umbrella Activities
mnemonics.content: PRQM: Project tracking, Risk management, Quality assurance, Configuration management, Measurement. Mnemonic: 'Please Review Quality Materials' or 'People Rarely Question Management'

mnemonics.title: Process Attributes
mnemonics.content: UVRMS: Understandability, Visibility, Robustness, Maintainability, Speed. Mnemonic: 'Uncle Victor Runs Main Street'

acronyms.usage: Describes the complete process of software development from requirements to deployment
Standard language for creating visual models of software systems
Framework for improving software development processes
Activity ensuring software meets quality standards

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a waterfall flowing from left to right: Communication (source) → Planning (channel) → Modeling (pools) → Construction (dam/reservoir) → Deployment (downstream)
Picture an umbrella covering all framework activities - they provide support and protection throughout the entire development process
Think of process patterns as reusable building blocks or LEGO pieces that can be assembled in different ways to build different processes
Visualize work products as layers being built - each layer (document, model, code) builds upon the previous one

analogies.analogy: A process model is like a recipe for cooking - it provides the ingredients (activities), steps (tasks), and expected outcome (software product)
Umbrella activities are like safety gear in construction - they protect the project throughout all phases, not just one
Iteration in software process is like writing an essay - you draft, review, revise, and repeat until the final product meets standards
Process tailoring is like customizing a car - you start with a base model and modify features to meet specific needs
